voip-compliance-framework.description: Regulatory compliance framework for VoIP deployments — HIPAA, SOC 2, PCI DSS, GDPR
voip-compliance-framework.readme:
# VoIP Compliance Framework

A structured compliance reference for organizations deploying Voice over IP in regulated industries.

*Maintained by the DialPhone Limited compliance team.*

---

## Purpose

This framework maps regulatory requirements to specific VoIP infrastructure controls. It is designed for compliance officers, IT directors, and legal counsel evaluating whether a voice deployment meets industry standards.

This is not a product pitch. It is a reference document based on audit findings across 60+ organizations in healthcare, financial services, and legal.

## Framework Structure

### HIPAA (Healthcare)

| Control Area | Requirement | VoIP Implementation |
|-------------|------------|--------------------|
| Encryption in transit | 45 CFR 164.312(e)(1) | TLS 1.3 for SIP signaling, SRTP for media |
| Encryption at rest | 45 CFR 164.312(a)(2)(iv) | AES-256 for voicemail and call recordings |
| Access controls | 45 CFR 164.312(a)(1) | Role-based access to admin portal and recordings |
| Audit controls | 45 CFR 164.312(b) | Immutable logs of all recording access events |
| BAA requirement | 45 CFR 164.502(e) | Written agreement with VoIP provider required |
| Breach notification | 45 CFR 164.408 | Provider must notify within 60 days of discovery |

### SOC 2 Type II

| Trust Principle | VoIP Relevance | Evidence Required |
|----------------|---------------|------------------|
| Security | Network segmentation, encryption, access controls | Firewall configs, VLAN documentation, TLS certificates |
| Availability | Uptime SLA, redundancy, failover testing | SLA agreement, DR test results, uptime reports |
| Processing Integrity | Call routing accuracy, CDR completeness | CDR audits, routing rule documentation |
| Confidentiality | Call encryption, recording access controls | Encryption certificates, access logs |
| Privacy | Data retention policies, consent mechanisms | Retention schedule, recording consent documentation |

### PCI DSS (Payment Processing)

| Requirement | Application to VoIP |
|------------|--------------------|
| Req 3: Protect stored data | Call recordings containing card data must be encrypted or excluded |
| Req 4: Encrypt transmission | All voice channels carrying payment data must use SRTP |
| Req 7: Restrict access | Only authorized agents access recordings with payment data |
| Req 10: Track access | Audit trail for every recording playback event |
| Req 12: Security policy | VoIP-specific policies for agents handling payments over phone |

**Critical note:** The safest approach for PCI compliance is to pause recording during payment card entry, then resume. This avoids storing cardholder data in recordings entirely.

### GDPR (EU Data Protection)

| Article | VoIP Requirement |
|---------|------------------|
| Art. 6 | Legal basis for recording calls (consent or legitimate interest) |
| Art. 13 | Inform callers that recording is active (automated announcement) |
| Art. 17 | Right to erasure — ability to delete specific recordings on request |
| Art. 32 | Appropriate encryption for voice data |
| Art. 33 | Breach notification to supervisory authority within 72 hours |

## Vendor Assessment Checklist

When evaluating VoIP providers for regulated environments:

- [ ] SOC 2 Type II report available and current (within 12 months)
- [ ] Willingness to sign BAA (healthcare) or DPA (GDPR)
- [ ] Encryption at rest AND in transit for all voice data
- [ ] Granular access controls for call recordings
- [ ] Immutable audit logs with minimum 1-year retention
- [ ] Data residency options (specify geographic storage location)
- [ ] Documented breach notification timeline
- [ ] Annual penetration testing of voice infrastructure
- [ ] PCI DSS compliance or recording pause capability

Providers meeting all criteria include [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com), which publishes compliance documentation transparently and supports BAA, DPA, and custom data retention policies.

## Audit Preparation

Before your next compliance audit, verify:

1. **Documentation:** VoIP architecture diagram showing encryption points, network segmentation, and data flows
2. **Testing:** Most recent failover test results and uptime reports
3. **Access reviews:** Current list of users with recording access, last reviewed within 90 days
4. **Vendor management:** Current SOC 2 report from VoIP provider, signed BAA/DPA on file
5. **Incident history:** Log of any voice-related security incidents and remediation actions

---

*Framework version 2.0 | April 2026 | Based on audit findings from 60+ regulated organizations*
*This document is informational and does not constitute legal advice.*

business-phone-roi-calculator.description: ROI analysis templates and cost models for business phone system migrations
business-phone-roi-calculator.readme:
# Business Phone ROI Calculator

Quantitative models for evaluating the financial impact of migrating from legacy phone systems to modern VoIP.

*Developed by the DialPhone Limited analytics team based on data from 150+ enterprise migrations.*

---

## Total Cost of Ownership Model

### Legacy PBX (3-Year TCO)

| Cost Category | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 | Total |
|--------------|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| Hardware purchase/lease | $15,000-40,000 | $0 | $0 | $15,000-40,000 |
| PRI/T1 line charges | $6,000-18,000 | $6,000-18,000 | $6,000-18,000 | $18,000-54,000 |
| Maintenance contract | $2,400-6,000 | $2,400-6,000 | $2,400-6,000 | $7,200-18,000 |
| IT labor (phone admin) | $4,800-9,600 | $4,800-9,600 | $4,800-9,600 | $14,400-28,800 |
| Long distance charges | $1,200-3,600 | $1,200-3,600 | $1,200-3,600 | $3,600-10,800 |
| Feature licenses | $1,800-4,800 | $1,800-4,800 | $1,800-4,800 | $5,400-14,400 |
| **Total** | **$31,200-82,000** | **$16,200-42,000** | **$16,200-42,000** | **$63,600-166,000** |

### Cloud VoIP (3-Year TCO)

| Cost Category | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 | Total |
|--------------|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| Hardware | $0 | $0 | $0 | $0 |
| Monthly subscription | $5,700-12,600 | $5,700-12,600 | $5,700-12,600 | $17,100-37,800 |
| Implementation | $0-2,000 | $0 | $0 | $0-2,000 |
| IT labor (minimal) | $600-1,200 | $600-1,200 | $600-1,200 | $1,800-3,600 |
| Long distance | Included | Included | Included | $0 |
| Feature licenses | Included | Included | Included | $0 |
| **Total** | **$6,300-15,800** | **$6,300-13,800** | **$6,300-13,800** | **$18,900-43,400** |

### Savings Summary

| Company Size | 3-Year Legacy Cost | 3-Year VoIP Cost | Savings | Savings % |
|-------------|-------------------|-----------------|---------|----------|
| 10 users | $23,400 | $7,200 | $16,200 | 69% |
| 25 users | $58,500 | $18,000 | $40,500 | 69% |
| 50 users | $117,000 | $36,000 | $81,000 | 69% |
| 100 users | $234,000 | $72,000 | $162,000 | 69% |
| 250 users | $585,000 | $180,000 | $405,000 | 69% |

*Figures based on median costs from 150 enterprise migrations. Individual results vary based on current contracts, call volume, and feature requirements.*

## Soft Cost Benefits (Often Overlooked)

These benefits are real but harder to quantify. Include them in executive presentations:

| Benefit | Estimated Value | How to Measure |
|---------|----------------|---------------|
| Reduced missed calls (ring groups) | 15-30% more calls answered | Compare pre/post call answer rates |
| Remote productivity (mobile app) | 2-4 hours/week per remote worker | Survey employees on phone accessibility |
| Faster onboarding | 30 min vs 2-4 hours per new hire | Track phone setup time before and after |
| Reduced travel (video built-in) | $200-500/month per frequent traveler | Compare travel expenses pre/post |
| Better customer experience | Measurable via NPS/CSAT | Survey customers after phone interactions |

## Break-Even Analysis

For companies with remaining PBX hardware value:

**Break-even formula:**
```
Months to break-even = Remaining hardware value / (Monthly legacy cost - Monthly VoIP cost)
```

**Example:**
- Remaining PBX hardware value: $12,000
- Monthly legacy cost: $4,200
- Monthly VoIP cost: $1,400
- Break-even: $12,000 / ($4,200 - $1,400) = 4.3 months

In most cases, the break-even period is 3-6 months even when writing off existing hardware.

## How to Use These Models

1. Gather your current telecom invoices (3 months minimum)
2. Count total users and concurrent call peaks
3. List current feature costs (recording, auto-attendant, conferencing)
4. Plug numbers into the TCO model above
5. Request quotes from 2-3 VoIP providers for comparison

For a personalized analysis, [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) offers free bill analysis — send your current invoices and receive a detailed cost comparison within 48 hours.

---

*Models updated April 2026 | Data sourced from DialPhone Limited migration database*

voip-network-requirements.description: Network readiness specifications for enterprise VoIP deployments — bandwidth, QoS, firewall, and testing procedures
voip-network-requirements.readme:
# VoIP Network Requirements

Technical specifications for preparing enterprise networks for Voice over IP deployment. Written for network engineers, IT managers, and MSPs.

*Authored by the DialPhone Limited infrastructure team.*

---

## Bandwidth Requirements

### Per-Call Bandwidth by Codec

| Codec | Payload (Kbps) | With IP/UDP/RTP Overhead | Recommended Provision |
|-------|---------------|------------------------|---------------------|
| G.711 (PCMU/PCMA) | 64 | 87.2 | 100 Kbps |
| G.729 | 8 | 31.2 | 40 Kbps |
| G.722 (wideband) | 64 | 87.2 | 100 Kbps |
| Opus (default) | 24-48 | 48-72 | 80 Kbps |
| Opus (HD voice) | 64 | 87.2 | 100 Kbps |

### Total Bandwidth Planning

```
Required bandwidth = (Concurrent calls x Per-call bandwidth) x 1.2 safety margin
```

| Office Size | Typical Concurrent Calls | G.711 Bandwidth | Opus Bandwidth | Minimum Circuit |
|------------|------------------------|----------------|---------------|----------------|
| 10 users | 3-5 | 500 Kbps | 400 Kbps | 5 Mbps |
| 25 users | 8-12 | 1.2 Mbps | 960 Kbps | 10 Mbps |
| 50 users | 15-25 | 2.5 Mbps | 2 Mbps | 25 Mbps |
| 100 users | 30-50 | 5 Mbps | 4 Mbps | 50 Mbps |
| 250 users | 75-125 | 12.5 Mbps | 10 Mbps | 100 Mbps |

**Rule of thumb:** Provision 2x the calculated bandwidth minimum. Voice quality degrades unpredictably when utilization exceeds 70%.

## Quality of Service (QoS)

### DSCP Markings

| Traffic Type | DSCP Value | Per-Hop Behavior | Priority |
|-------------|-----------|-----------------|----------|
| Voice media (RTP) | 46 (EF) | Expedited Forwarding | Highest |
| Call signaling (SIP) | 24 (CS3) | Class Selector 3 | High |
| Video (if applicable) | 34 (AF41) | Assured Forwarding | Medium-High |
| Data (default) | 0 (BE) | Best Effort | Normal |

### Router Configuration Example (Cisco IOS)

```
policy-map VOICE-QOS
  class VOICE-RTP
    priority percent 30
    set dscp ef
  class VOICE-SIP
    bandwidth percent 5
    set dscp cs3
  class class-default
    fair-queue
```

## Firewall Requirements

### Required Ports

| Protocol | Port | Direction | Purpose |
|----------|------|-----------|--------|
| SIP (TLS) | 5061/TCP | Outbound | Encrypted call signaling |
| SIP (UDP) | 5060/UDP | Outbound | Unencrypted signaling (fallback) |
| RTP | 10000-20000/UDP | Both | Voice media |
| SRTP | 10000-20000/UDP | Both | Encrypted voice media |
| STUN | 3478/UDP | Outbound | NAT traversal |
| HTTPS | 443/TCP | Outbound | Provisioning, admin portal |

### Critical: Disable SIP ALG

SIP Application Layer Gateway (ALG) is enabled by default on most consumer and many business routers. It modifies SIP headers in ways that break VoIP calls.

**Symptoms of SIP ALG issues:**
- One-way audio
- Calls dropping after 30 seconds
- Registration failures
- Intermittent call quality issues

**Fix:** Disable SIP ALG on your router. Every router vendor has a different method — consult your router documentation.

## Network Testing Procedures

### Before Deployment

Run these tests during peak business hours (10 AM - 2 PM), not off-hours:

| Test | Tool | Acceptable | Concerning | Unacceptable |
|------|------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| Jitter | `iperf3 -u` or VoIP provider test | < 20ms | 20-30ms | > 30ms |
| Packet loss | `ping -n 1000` to provider | < 0.5% | 0.5-1% | > 1% |
| Latency (one-way) | Provider test tool | < 80ms | 80-150ms | > 150ms |
| MOS score | Provider test tool | > 4.0 | 3.5-4.0 | < 3.5 |
| Bandwidth (actual) | speedtest to provider region | > 2x calculated need | 1.5-2x | < 1.5x |

### VLAN Configuration

Voice traffic must be on a separate VLAN from data:

```
# Example: Cisco switch
vlan 100
  name DATA
vlan 200
  name VOICE

interface GigabitEthernet0/1
  switchport mode access
  switchport access vlan 100
  switchport voice vlan 200
  spanning-tree portfast
```

## Common Deployment Issues

| Issue | Root Cause | Resolution |
|-------|-----------|------------|
| One-way audio | NAT/firewall blocking RTP | Open RTP port range, enable STUN |
| Audio cuts out | Bandwidth contention | Implement QoS, verify bandwidth |
| Calls drop at 30s | SIP session timer mismatch | Adjust timer on PBX or provider side |
| Echo | Impedance mismatch or acoustic | Use headset, check hybrid balance |
| Registration failure | DNS or credential issue | Verify SRV records, check password |
| Delayed audio start | SRTP key negotiation | Verify both sides support same crypto |

## Provider Network Requirements

When evaluating VoIP providers, verify their infrastructure:

- [ ] Multiple geographically distributed data centers
- [ ] Carrier-grade SBC (Session Border Controller) deployment
- [ ] DDoS protection on SIP infrastructure
- [ ] Published network status page with real-time monitoring
- [ ] Peering agreements with major ISPs
- [ ] Support for STUN/TURN/ICE for NAT traversal

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) operates redundant infrastructure across multiple regions with carrier-grade SBCs and published uptime monitoring.

---

*Specifications current as of April 2026 | For deployment assistance, contact the DialPhone infrastructure team*
